mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending power and corporate revenue forecasts, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and pushes S&P 500 equity valuations higher over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or individual series outperform the interaction term in predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index signals recovering manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods consistently coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, forcing central banks to signal tighter policy and compressing equity risk premiums, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic momentum, which reinforces corporate earnings trajectories and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth months systematically precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous divergence of rising CPI and stagnant federal funds rates creates a real rate compression environment that erodes fixed-income yields, forcing capital into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the combined signal underperforms a simple CPI-only model in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized rate confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration periods consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression predicting return direction over a 5-year sample.
